title: Apothecary Coffee Table with Toybox Trundle
summary: A planked 1x6 coffee table with a rolling toybox trundle underneath, its front dressed in fourteen false drawer faces for a genuine apothecary-cabinet look over what's really one big pull-out bin.
description:
The coffee table itself is a plain frame: four 2x2 legs joined by long stretchers, boxed in with 1x6 side boards at each end, and topped with five 1x6 planks run tight for a solid plank look — **the top boards are shortened slightly from a straight measurement so they don't overlap the legs**, so don't just rip five boards to the frame's outer dimension.

The trundle is a shallow 1x12 box on casters, sized to slide in and out from under the coffee table — mount the caster blocks low and check the caster clearance (about 2" typically) against the coffee table's own leg height before finalizing the trundle's box height, or it won't roll under cleanly.

**Fourteen thin false drawer fronts applied across the trundle's face are what give this piece its apothecary-cabinet look** — they're decorative panels, not real drawers opening individually; the whole trundle pulls out as one unit while the fronts make it read as a wall of small drawers. Space them evenly in a grid and add a knob to each for a convincing multi-drawer look.

materials.name: Hobby stock or 1/4" plywood for false drawer fronts
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to the length of your own leg sides.
Shortened to avoid overlapping the legs.
Cut to the width of your tabletop.

cutList.material: Hobby stock or 1/4" plywood for false drawer fronts

steps.title: Build the table frame
steps.body: Join the four legs with the stretchers and end box boards into the coffee table base.

steps.title: Add the side trim and cross stretchers
steps.body: Fit and attach the side trim, then attach the cross stretchers sized to your tabletop's actual width.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the five top boards to the frame, tight against each other.

steps.title: Build the trundle box
steps.body: Assemble the trundle's long sides and short ends into a shallow box, then add the trim.

steps.title: Mount the casters
steps.body: Attach the caster mounting blocks and casters to the bottom of the trundle, checking clearance against the coffee table's leg height.

steps.title: Add the false drawer fronts
steps.body: Space and attach the fourteen false drawer fronts evenly across the trundle's face, then add a knob to each.
materials: Hobby stock or 1/4" plywood for false drawer fronts

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Apothecary style coffee table with rolling toybox trundle
